name: Alex Jones
description: Alex Jones is an American far-right conspiracy theorist and radio show host. He is the host of The Alex Jones Show, broadcast from Austin, Texas, which airs on the Genesis Communications Network and online. Jones is known for promoting conspiracy theories and misinformation. His theories often involve claims of government conspiracies, secret societies, and globalist plots. He has been criticized for spreading falsehoods, inciting violence, and promoting hate speech. Jones' career began in the 1990s, gaining traction with his coverage of events like the Waco siege and the Oklahoma City bombing. He frequently presents alternative explanations for major events, often contradicting mainstream narratives and established facts. Mainstream media and scholarly sources consistently debunk Jones' claims, highlighting factual inaccuracies, logical fallacies, and a lack of credible evidence. His influence extends to a significant audience, demonstrating the power of misinformation and the appeal of conspiracy theories in contemporary society. He has faced legal consequences for his statements, most notably related to his false claims about the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting.
subtitle: Far-right conspiracy theorist and radio host

claims.claim: The Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
claims.evidence: Jones and his media outlets presented various claims suggesting the shooting was staged, citing inconsistencies in official reports and alleged crisis actors.
claims.criticism: This claim has been widely debunked by law enforcement, the media, and the victims' families. Jones was found liable for defamation and ordered to pay substantial damages to the families for the emotional distress caused by his false statements.

claims.claim: The government was involved in the 9/11 attacks.
claims.evidence: Jones has presented various theories suggesting that the U.S. government either allowed the attacks to happen or directly orchestrated them, citing alleged anomalies in the collapse of the World Trade Center towers and the lack of a definitive explanation for the Pentagon attack.
claims.criticism: The 9/11 Commission Report, extensive scientific analysis, and eyewitness accounts overwhelmingly support the conclusion that the attacks were carried out by al-Qaeda. Jones' claims are based on speculation and misinterpretations of evidence.

claims.claim: There is a globalist plot to create a New World Order.
claims.evidence: Jones frequently discusses a supposed secret cabal of powerful individuals and organizations seeking to establish a totalitarian world government, citing events like international treaties and global economic institutions as evidence.
claims.criticism: The concept of a New World Order is a broad conspiracy theory with no factual basis. Mainstream analysis views international cooperation and global institutions as efforts to address shared challenges, not as evidence of a secret plot.

keyFigures.name: Alex Jones
keyFigures.role: Host of The Alex Jones Show and primary promoter of conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Anderson Cooper
keyFigures.role: CNN anchor who has interviewed Jones and challenged his claims, providing a platform for rebuttal.

timeline.event: Alex Jones begins his radio career in Austin, Texas.
Jones gains notoriety for his coverage of the Waco siege and the Oklahoma City bombing.
Jones begins promoting conspiracy theories about the 9/11 attacks.
The Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting occurs; Jones begins promoting false claims about the event.
Jones is found liable for defamation and ordered to pay damages to the Sandy Hook families.

sources.author: Will Sommer
sources.work: Trust the Plan: The Rise of QAnon and the Conspiracy That Unhinged America
sources.title: Will Sommer

sources.author: Carlos Maza
sources.work: Why I'm done being polite to Alex Jones
sources.title: Carlos Maza

sources.author: Elizabeth Williamson
sources.work: Sandy Hook: An American Tragedy and the Battle for Truth
sources.title: Elizabeth Williamson

worshipPractices.name: The Infowars Ritual
worshipPractices.description: Followers gather daily to consume hours of unfiltered pronouncements, often involving the repetition of key phrases and slogans. This is believed to build immunity against the 'globalist mind control' and reveal hidden truths in plain sight, however contradictory.

worshipPractices.name: Supplement Sacrifice
worshipPractices.description: Devotees purchase and consume large quantities of dietary supplements advertised during broadcasts, believing they possess special protective properties. The act is seen as a financial offering and a physical commitment to resisting perceived threats to bodily autonomy.

festivals.name: The Day of Reckoning
festivals.timing: Whenever a major news event occurs
festivals.description: Followers interpret current events through the lens of pre-existing conspiracy theories, often identifying hidden actors and sinister plots. This involves feverish online discussions, meme creation, and the proclamation of impending doom or triumph, depending on the interpretation.

festivals.name: The Anti-Globalist Jamboree
festivals.description: A large gathering of believers where they share information, network, and reaffirm their commitment to fighting against perceived enemies. Speakers amplify popular narratives, vendors sell related merchandise, and attendees engage in demonstrations against perceived injustices.

cultural.modernLegacy: The influence of Alex Jones is evident in the proliferation of online conspiracy theories and the mainstreaming of extremist viewpoints. His approach has inspired similar figures and contributed to a climate of distrust in traditional media and institutions, leaving a lasting impact on the media landscape and political discourse. His techniques of spreading misinformation are also used in literature and art as a reflection of modern society.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Loki is a trickster god often spreading misinformation and chaos amongst the Aesir, similar to Alex Jones' promotion of conspiracy theories that sow discord.
Pheme is the personification of rumor and gossip, known for spreading unverified and often exaggerated information, much like Alex Jones' dissemination of conspiracy theories.

Narada is a wandering sage known for carrying tales between gods and humans, sometimes instigating conflict and discord through the spread of information, even if not always malicious, mirroring the way Jones can incite strong reactions.

Anansi, a spider trickster figure, often uses cunning and deception to achieve his goals, spinning elaborate tales that, while entertaining, can distort reality, similar to Alex Jones' use of narrative to promote his views.

associations.name: Serpent
associations.significance: Associated with spreading deceitful narratives and tempting with forbidden knowledge, mirroring Jones's promotion of conspiracy theories.

associations.name: Chaos
associations.significance: Represents the disruption and instability Jones's rhetoric often incites within the public discourse.

associations.name: Rust
associations.significance: Symbolizes decay and the corruption of truth, reflecting the degradation of information Jones propagates.
A number traditionally associated with the Antichrist and ultimate evil, reflecting the extreme and often malicious nature of his rhetoric.

associations.name: Fire
associations.significance: Represents the passionate, destructive, and often uncontrollable nature of Jones's pronouncements, easily igniting fear and anger.

associations.name: Pandora's Box
associations.significance: Represents the unintended and harmful consequences unleashed by the dissemination of conspiracy theories and misinformation.

associations.name: Discord
associations.significance: Eris, the Greek goddess of discord, is a personification of strife and disagreement, reflecting the divisiveness that Jones's rhetoric creates within society.

companions.name: Apep
companions.description: Apep is the ancient Egyptian serpent deity who embodies chaos and darkness. Alex Jones might see Apep as a kindred spirit in the fight against the established order and those whom he perceives as spreading misinformation.

companions.name: Typhon
companions.description: Typhon is a monstrous serpentine giant in Greek mythology, known for his destructive power and defiance of the Olympian gods. Jones might align with Typhon's anti-establishment stance and his chaotic nature.

companions.name: Jormungandr
companions.description: Jormungandr, the Midgard Serpent, is a massive sea serpent in Norse mythology that encircles the world and is destined to battle Thor during Ragnarok. Jones might see Jormungandr as a symbol of the hidden forces at play in the world.

companions.name: Set
companions.description: Set is the Egyptian god of chaos, storms, and the desert, often associated with disruption and foreign lands. Jones might find common ground with Set's rebellious nature and opposition to the established order.
